title: salix nummularia subsp. tundricola
intro: Salix nummularia subsp. tundricola, commonly known as the tundra willow or Alaskan willow, is a subspecies of shrub or small tree that belongs to the willow family and is native to the Arctic regions of North America. The medicinal properties of this plant lie in its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, providing anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects. The health benefits of Salix nummularia subsp. tundricola include its use in reducing fever, alleviating headaches, and treating various types of pain, as well as its potential anti-diabetic properties. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas or infusions, can be made by steeping the bark or leaves in hot water, offering a natural remedy for various health issues.
